description.current: Validate APIs via curl: health checks, CRUD cycles (create/read/update/delete), auth (token, 401/403), error responses, pagination. Captures JSON bodies and status codes. Use on API changes.
issues: Missing 'when to use' context in description itself — 'Use on API changes' is vague. Should include 'before deployment', 'after auth changes', 'when adding endpoints'
Doesn't explain what 'validation' means here: confirming API contract correctness (schemas, status codes, error structures), not just 'the API responds'

issues.description: Repetitive curl patterns (lines 27-95): every CRUD operation follows curl → tee → jq → check format. Same 30-line pattern repeated 7 times. Should be bundled as scripts/crud-validator.sh with arguments for resource name

No clear entry point: skill jumps to Prerequisites (lines 14-23) then Step 1. User doesn't know if they should run all 6 steps or pick a subset. No guidance on priority (health check > CRUD > error handling, or something else?)
Prerequisites table (lines 14-23) lists 5 requirements but doesn't say 'STOP here if any fail' or link to how to fix (e.g., 'database seeding' — how? See what docs?)
Error response validation (lines 144-168) is good but abstract: 'Test that error responses include proper status codes AND meaningful bodies' without examples of GOOD vs BAD responses

CRUD cycle section (lines 34-98) mixes instructions with bash code that is neither runnable as-is (needs PORT, TOKEN, RESOURCE interpolation) nor properly templated (variables appear undefined mid-example)
Step 6 (Rate Limiting) is optional ('if applicable') but no guidance on when to include it or how to assess if rate limiting exists in the API
Common Failures table (lines 210-219) is useful but comes AFTER all steps; should come after Prerequisites so users can diagnose failures early

strengths: PASS Criteria template (lines 221-232) is excellent — 10 concrete checkboxes, scannable, actionable
Health Check step (lines 25-32) is concise and includes warning: 'bare 200 OK without body is suspicious'
Evidence capture reminder (line 204-208) is clear: 'Every error response MUST include a human-readable message, not just a status code'
Step 5 (Pagination) includes key verification checks: different items per page, consistent totals, empty page handling
Common Failures table shows SYMPTOM → CAUSE → FIX mapping (lines 210-219), practical for debugging

bundled_resources.missing_scripts_opportunity: CRUD cycle pattern (lines 34-98) repeated 7x — should be scripts/crud-validator.sh that takes resource name, endpoint base, and token; one-liner execution per resource. Auth testing (lines 100-142) should be scripts/auth-test.sh. Error response validation (lines 144-168) should be scripts/error-response-test.sh.

top_fixes: Add 'When to Use' section after description: 'Before any deployment, after adding/modifying auth, after endpoint changes, when onboarding new API consumers. Validates contract correctness, not just reachability.'

Restructure to clear flow: (1) Prerequisites (unchanged). (2) QUICK START: 'Run these in order: Health Check (5 min) → CRUD (20 min) → Auth (10 min). If all PASS, stop here. Otherwise, continue to Error Responses and Pagination.' (3) Then detailed steps.
Move Common Failures table (lines 210-219) to come immediately after Prerequisites as 'Troubleshooting'

Create scripts/crud-validator.sh: takes RESOURCE, ENDPOINT_BASE, TOKEN as args, runs create/read/update/delete/verify cycle once, outputs all responses to e2e-evidence/api-crud-$RESOURCE/. Replace lines 34-98 with: 'Run: bash scripts/crud-validator.sh posts http://localhost:3000/api $TOKEN. Evidence saves to e2e-evidence/api-crud-posts/'

Create scripts/auth-test.sh: takes ENDPOINT_BASE, EMAIL, PASSWORD, returns TOKEN if valid. Replace lines 100-142 with: 'Run: TOKEN=$(bash scripts/auth-test.sh http://localhost:3000 user@ex.com pwd). Test protected routes: curl -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" [endpoint]'
Expand Error Response section (lines 144-168) with good/bad examples: 'GOOD: {"error": "Invalid email format", "field": "email"}. BAD: {"error": 1008} (numeric code only, no message)'
Clarify Step 6 (Rate Limiting): add decision tree — 'Does API docs mention rate limits? If yes, add this step. If no, skip.'
Rewrite Prerequisites to 'STOP before proceeding:' — make it a gate, not just info

test_prompts.prompt: I have a Node.js API at localhost:3000 with /api/posts endpoints. I need to validate the full CRUD cycle (create, read, update, delete) and capture evidence. How do I run the API validation? What evidence should I capture?
test_prompts.expected_output: Run Health Check first: curl -s http://localhost:3000/health | jq . | tee e2e-evidence/api-health.json. Then CRUD: create a post, extract ID, read it back, update it, verify update persisted, delete it, verify 404. Save each response to e2e-evidence/api-crud-posts/. Evidence is JSON bodies + status codes. No response body = FAIL.

test_prompts.prompt: Our API uses JWT tokens. The login endpoint returns {"access_token": "..."}. How do I test that auth is working correctly — both valid tokens and invalid/missing tokens?
test_prompts.expected_output: Get token: TOKEN=$(curl -s -X POST http://localhost:3000/auth/login -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d '{"email":"...","password":"..."}' | jq -r '.access_token'). Test valid: curl -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" http://localhost:3000/api/protected (expect 200). Test invalid: curl -H 'Authorization: Bearer invalid.token' http://localhost:3000/api/protected (expect 401). Test missing: curl http://localhost:3000/api/protected (expect 401).

test_prompts.prompt: I'm testing error responses. When I POST to /api/posts with an empty body ({}), the API returns HTTP 422 with just {"errors": []}. Is this correct? What should the error response look like?
test_prompts.expected_output: No. An empty errors array is not useful. Each error should include field name and description: {"errors": [{"field": "title", "message": "Title is required"}, {"field": "body", "message": "Body is required"}]}. Your API needs to populate the errors array with specific, actionable messages. This is a FAIL until the API is fixed.

test_prompts.prompt: After running the CRUD validation, I want to know if I covered all the resources in the API. How do I know if I've tested enough?
test_prompts.expected_output: Run CRUD for each resource endpoint in your API: /api/posts, /api/comments, /api/users, etc. PASS Criteria checkbox: Create returns 201 with ID, Read returns 200 with same data, Update persists, Delete returns 404 on re-read. If all endpoints PASS these 4 checks, you've covered the API contract. Pagination and rate limiting are optional extras; focus on core CRUD first.
